source.title: Emergency Disconnect Sequence - Noble Stanley Lafosse Drillship

context.region: Gulf of America, Mississippi Canyon Block 300

actions_taken: Yellow alert status declared due to excursion greater than 5 meters
Red alert declared when K-POSS panel indicated critical status change
Emergency Disconnect Sequence 3 activated from bridge
Upper and lower blind shear rams closed
ROV deployed to assess LMRP and LBOP condition
Red watch circle of 48 meters established
event.summary: On May 29, 2025, at approximately 10:46 a.m., the Noble Stanley Lafosse Drillship experienced an unexpected change in wind speed and direction due to strong thunderstorms while preparing for cementing operations at Mississippi Canyon Block 300. Wind gusts reached 60 knots with waterspouts impacting the vessel, causing a 14-meter excursion while hanging off 5-7/8 inch drill pipe. The crew initiated an Emergency Disconnect Sequence, successfully closing blind shear rams and securing the well. The incident resulted in the discharge of 417 barrels of 10.9 ppg calcium chloride brine to the sea. No injuries were reported.
recommendations: Refine operational readiness during critical phases

threats.name: Severe Weather Event - Rapid Wind Changes

consequences.name: Discharge of Drilling Fluids to Sea

controls.name: Weather Monitoring and Forecasting System
controls.barrier_role: Detection and early warning of severe weather conditions
controls.lod_basis: First line of defense against weather-related threats through monitoring and prediction

supporting_text: Weather monitoring tools were in use, and radar did indicate nearby storm activity; however, the most recent forecast did not predict the severity of the conditions encountered
the latest weather report received from DTN at 6:14 AM on May 29th did not indicate the potential for 60 knot winds
Forecasts had indicated winds not exceeding 10 knots; however, a storm developed, producing a sudden wind gust of 60 knots

controls.name: Dynamic Positioning System
controls.barrier_role: Maintain vessel position within operational limits
controls.lod_basis: Primary system to prevent loss of station-keeping

supporting_text: rapid and variable wind conditions that exceeded the rig's maneuvering and station-keeping capacity
Multiple changes in wind direction caused the wind to impact the beam of the vessel, generating significant lateral forces that exceeded the rig's station-keeping capability
the rig was experiencing a 14-meter excursion while hanging off 5-7/8 inch drill pipe

controls.name: K-POSS Position Monitoring System
controls.barrier_role: Monitor vessel position and alert crew to excursions
controls.lod_basis: Secondary monitoring and alarm system to detect position deviations

supporting_text: the rig entered yellow alert status due to an excursion greater than 5 meters
a red alert was declared when the K-POSS panel indicated a critical status change

controls.name: Well-Specific Operating Guidelines
controls.barrier_role: Define operational limits and response procedures
controls.lod_basis: Primary procedural control defining safe operating envelope

supporting_text: the crew responded swiftly and in full compliance with established procedures

controls.name: Emergency Disconnect Sequence Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Safely disconnect riser and secure well when position limits exceeded
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to prevent well control loss and major environmental damage

supporting_text: the command to disconnect was issued from the bridge and promptly executed on the rig floor
Emergency Disconnect Sequence 3 was activated, successfully closing both the upper and lower blind shear rams
the SDPO demonstrated sound judgment by escalating the vessel's status to DP Red Alert and initiating the EDS before breaching the red watch circle

controls.name: Blind Shear Rams (Upper and Lower)
controls.barrier_role: Physically sever drill pipe and seal wellbore to prevent well control loss
controls.lod_basis: Ultimate physical barrier to secure well and prevent blowout

supporting_text: Emergency Disconnect Sequence 3 was activated, successfully closing both the upper and lower blind shear rams
The well is secured with appropriate barriers in place

controls.name: Cement Isolation Barrier Plug
controls.barrier_role: Provide well barrier to prevent flow from formation
controls.lod_basis: Secondary well barrier independent of surface equipment

supporting_text: while the Noble Stanley Lafosse was preparing to pump cement after successfully setting a Cement Isolation Barrier Plug (CIBP) at 20,602 feet RKB

controls.name: Red Watch Circle
controls.barrier_role: Define maximum allowable vessel excursion before mandatory disconnect
controls.lod_basis: Secondary procedural limit to trigger emergency response

supporting_text: A red watch circle of 48 meters was established for the SS003 well
initiating the EDS before breaching the red watch circle

controls.name: ROV Inspection of Subsea Equipment
controls.barrier_role: Assess integrity of subsea equipment after incident
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident recovery action to verify equipment integrity

supporting_text: a Remotely Operated Vehicle (ROV) was deployed to assess the condition of the Lower Marine Riser Package (LMRP) and Lower Blowout Preventer (LBOP)

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
